name: First Bull Run
note: Union rout; wounded flooded back through Philadelphia

name: Ball's Bluff
note: Philadelphia units among the dead; 49th PA and 20th MA suffered heavily

name: Fort Henry
note: First Union victory in the Western Theater opened the Tennessee River

name: Pea Ridge
note: Union victory secured Missouri — fought the same day the 93d and 103d NY passed through Philadelphia

name: Shiloh
note: Two-day battle shocked the country; 23,000 casualties total

name: Mechanicsville (Seven Days)
note: Opening of the Seven Days Battles; Union retreat from the Peninsula began

name: Second Bull Run
note: Second Union disaster near Washington; shook Northern confidence

name: Antietam
note: Bloodiest single day of the war; Emancipation Proclamation issued five days later

name: Fredericksburg
note: Catastrophic Union frontal assault; 12,600 Union casualties in one afternoon

name: Chancellorsville
note: Lee's most celebrated victory; Stonewall Jackson mortally wounded by his own men

name: Gettysburg
note: Confederate high-water mark — 50 miles from Philadelphia; Cooper Shop sheltered thousands of wounded in the days after

name: Vicksburg Surrenders
note: Union controls the Mississippi River; the Confederacy is split

name: Chickamauga
note: Second-deadliest battle of the war; Union narrowly avoids rout

name: The Wilderness
note: Grant's first engagement against Lee; dense forest fighting, heavy casualties on both sides

name: Cold Harbor
note: Costly Union frontal assault; Grant later called it his one regret of the war

name: Atlanta Falls
note: Sherman captures Atlanta; Lincoln's re-election is secured
